summary: TypeInitializationException with inner DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp when using SkiaSharp 2.88.3 in a Blazor WebAssembly app on Windows, with a recent report of the same error on SkiaSharp 3.119.0 with .NET 8.

stepsToReproduce: Add SkiaSharp and SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly NuGet packages (2.88.3)
Run the app — DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows 11, Visual Studio, SkiaSharp 2.88.3 (OP). Also reported on 3.119.0 with .NET 8 (comment #3).

bugSignals.errorMessage: The type initializer for 'SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo' threw an exception — inner: DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The original report was on 2.88.3 which had known WASM packaging gaps. PR #3082 (merged 2024-11-27) moved the Blazor workaround into WASM for 3.x. Comment #3 claims 3.119.0 still fails but provides no project configuration details to confirm.

fixStatus.reason: PR #3082 (merged 2024-11-27) fixed WASM native asset packaging for 3.x, and the .targets file has explicit net8.0 support. However, comment #3 reports the same error on 3.119.0 with .NET 8, suggesting either a configuration problem or an incomplete fix.

analysis.summary: DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp in Blazor WASM occurs because WASM uses static linking — .a files must be compiled into dotnet.wasm at build time via NativeFileReference. In 2.x, this required manual configuration. PR #3082 improved 3.x packaging, but a recent comment claims it still fails on 3.119.0.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because the native library fails to load in a supported platform configuration. Area is area/libSkiaSharp.native because the root cause is native asset packaging/linking for WASM, not a C# API issue. The maintainer already provided guidance for 2.x users and noted 3.x should work out of the box with PR #3082. The latest comment (3.119.0 failure) needs investigation to determine if it's a real bug or misconfiguration.

keySignals.interpretation: Classic WASM static linking failure — the .a files were not compiled into dotnet.wasm at build time.

keySignals.text: For 2.x series, there are a few issues with WebAssembly and the fact we don't automatically include a lot of things.
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer acknowledges known WASM packaging gaps in 2.x and recommends 3.x.

keySignals.text: Also running into this issue using the latest version (3.119.0) with .NET8.
keySignals.interpretation: Potential unresolved bug even after PR #3082, but no project configuration provided to diagnose.

codeInvestigation.finding: The static constructor calls SkiaApi.sk_colortype_get_default_8888() which triggers P/Invoke into libSkiaSharp. On WASM, this requires the native library to be statically linked into dotnet.wasm — if not linked, TypeInitializationException wraps DllNotFoundException.

NativeFileReference items are conditionally included based on TargetFrameworkVersion (net6.0, net7.0, net8.0, net9.0+). Requires UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly or UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly to be true. If neither is set, no .a files are linked.
SkiaSharpStaticLibraryPath is set relative to MSBuildThisFileDirectory. All .a files under this path are collected into SkiaSharpStaticLibrary item group.

Blazor views project references SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly as a ProjectReference, which auto-includes the .targets for consumers. Using SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor should automatically set up WASM native linking.

workarounds: For 2.x: Manually add NativeFileReference in the project file pointing to the .a files from SkiaSharpStaticLibraryPath
Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x and use SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor which auto-includes NativeAssets.WebAssembly
nextQuestions: Does the 3.119.0 reporter (comment #3) use SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor or reference NativeAssets.WebAssembly directly?
Is the reporter's project using the Blazor WebAssembly SDK (UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly) or a different SDK?
Are the .a files actually present in the NuGet package cache at the expected path?

resolution.hypothesis: The original 2.x issue is a known limitation — WASM native assets were not auto-configured. For the 3.119.0 report, the cause is likely misconfiguration (not using SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor, or using SkiaSharp directly without NativeAssets.WebAssembly).

proposals.title: Upgrade to 3.x with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor
proposals.description: Use SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.x which auto-includes NativeAssets.WebAssembly and configures NativeFileReference via .targets. This is the recommended path and should work out of the box for Blazor WASM apps.

proposals.title: Manual NativeFileReference for 2.x
proposals.description: For users staying on 2.x, manually reference the static libraries in the project file as mattleibow described in comment #2.

proposals.title: Investigate 3.119.0 failure
proposals.description: Request project configuration details from the 3.119.0 reporter to determine if it's a packaging bug or misconfiguration. Key questions: which packages are referenced, which SDK is used, is WasmBuildNative enabled.

resolution.recommendedReason: The original 2.x issue has a known workaround (upgrade to 3.x). The critical open question is whether 3.119.0 truly fails — if confirmed, it indicates an incomplete fix in PR #3082.

actionability.reason: The 2.x issue is understood and has workarounds. The 3.119.0 report (comment #3) needs reproduction to determine if it's a real bug or misconfiguration.

actions.description: Apply bug, native, and WASM labels
Request configuration details from the 3.119.0 reporter and provide guidance
actions.comment:
Thanks for the reports.

For anyone on SkiaSharp 2.x: WebAssembly uses static linking, not dynamic P/Invoke — the `.a` files must be compiled into `dotnet.wasm` at build time. In 2.x this requires manual configuration as described in the comment above. The recommended path is upgrading to SkiaSharp 3.x with `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor`, which auto-includes the native assets.

@tolzy88 — could you share your project file (`.csproj`)? Specifically:
- Which SkiaSharp packages do you reference? (`SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` vs just `SkiaSharp` + `NativeAssets.WebAssembly`)
- Are you using the Blazor WebAssembly SDK (`Microsoft.NET.Sdk.BlazorWebAssembly`)?
- Is `WasmBuildNative` set in your project?

PR #3082 fixed the WASM native asset packaging for 3.x. If you're using `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` and still seeing this, we'll need to dig deeper.
